pattern: Normalizing each domino to a canonical sorted key collapses rotations into one bucket, so counting pairs reduces to summing k-choose-2 over each bucket's frequency.
hints: Two dominoes match if one is a rotation of the other, so a domino and its flip should be treated as the same thing.
Sort the two halves of each domino to make a canonical key, then group dominoes by that key.
Count how many dominoes fall in each key bucket; a bucket of size c contributes c*(c-1)/2 pairs, summed over all buckets.

edge_inputs.description: one rotated pair among distinct others
mix of identical and rotated forms counted together
symmetric domino equals only itself when duplicated
all rotations of the same domino

stress.description: many dominoes over the small 1..9 value domain so buckets grow large
stress.note: limited value range forces frequent equivalences
